(Eurocrypt 2023) and follows the BMR template, assuming the Decisional Composite Residuosity for the garbling phase and Learning Parity with Noise for preprocessing.

We evaluate our protocol on matrix-vector multiplication, a fundamental operation for data analysis. For standard computation parameters, we reduce communication bandwidth by $101\times$ to $247\times$ and improves end-to-end runtime by $4.4\times$ to $10.7\times$ compared to state-of-the-art constant-round Boolean MPC baselines, even when accounting for the overhead of a full bit-decomposition on the output vector.

item.title: The ABC of Symmetric Primitives over Integer Rings: Milk Before Meat
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1104

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

传统对称密码设计多基于有限域 $\mathbb{F}_{p^n}^t$（如AES的$\mathbb{F}_{2^8}$），其代数结构清晰、攻击模型成熟。然而，面向同态加密、安全多方计算等新兴场景，整数环 $\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$ 上的对称原语日益重要——因其天然支持模幂、加法同态及高效大整数运算。但该领域缺乏系统性理论支撑：已有环上密码（如早期$\mathbb{Z}_{2^n}$分组密码）屡遭快速攻破，根源在于$\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$与$\mathbb{F}_{p^n}^t$存在本质差异——前者含零因子、非域、多项式表示不唯一，导致经典差分/线性/代数攻击无法直接迁移。

方法与创新

本文首次构建整数环上对称密码设计的严格基础框架。我们：

  • 系统评估现有统计与代数攻击（如高阶差分、多项式插值）在$\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$下的有效性边界；
  • 提出两类新攻击：零因子传播攻击（利用非单位元乘法坍塌特性）与模降阶代数攻击（通过模$p$约化暴露低阶结构）；
  • 关键发现：S盒是否具有多项式表示（即能否写成$\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}[x]$中多项式）直接影响安全性——多项式S盒易受代数攻击，而非多项式S盒可天然抵抗此类威胁；
  • 定义两个新度量指标：零因子敏感度（Zero-Divisor Sensitivity）与模降阶鲁棒性（Mod-Reduction Robustness），量化非多项式S盒的抗攻击能力；
  • 给出两种实用构造：1）基于逐位数字操作（digit manipulation）的非多项式S盒（如对$p$进制各位独立置换后重组）；2）基于不可约多项式复合+模截断的混合构造。

意义

本工作 bridging theory and practice：既为环上密码提供首个可验证的安全准则，又产出可直接部署的S盒设计工具，推动隐私计算底层原语的标准化。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes the first rigorous foundation for designing secure symmetric primitives over integer rings $\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}^t$, motivated by applications in homomorphic encryption and MPC. We analyze the failure of standard attacks (e.g., differential, linear, algebraic) in this setting due to structural differences from fields—especially zero divisors and non-unique polynomial representations. We introduce two novel attacks exploiting these features: zero-divisor propagation and mod-reduction algebraic attacks. Crucially, we show that non-polynomial S-boxes—functions not representable as polynomials over $\mathbb{Z}_{p^n}$—exhibit inherent resistance; we formalize this via two new security metrics: Zero-Divisor Sensitivity and Mod-Reduction Robustness. Finally, we provide two concrete constructions of such S-boxes, including one based on digit-wise manipulation in base-$p$ representation. Our framework bridges theory and practice, enabling both security proofs and efficient implementations.

Abstract

Designing a secure symmetric-key cipher over a vector space over a field $\mathbb F_{p^n}^t$ is well known and understood by the cryptographic community. Even if the attacks are continuously improving, our current understanding regarding the design and security of the majority of the symmetric-key primitives has not fundamentally changed in the last 20 years.

How does this picture change when we move to an integer ring $\mathbb Z_{p^n}^t$? Although the question is easy to state, it turns out to be far harder to answer. Indeed, there is a significant difference between the arithmetics of $\mathbb F_{p^n}^t$ and $\mathbb Z_{p^n}^t$ and attack vectors do not apply/translate directly between the two. As a case in point, a few ciphers have already been designed over integer rings, yet their initial versions have already been broken.

In this paper, we lay the foundations for a more rigorous approach to designing ciphers over integer rings, noting that this is not only of theoretical interest, but also has concrete applications. We analyze how existing statistical and algebraic attacks will behave for these ciphers and also present new attacks that take into account that not all functions over integer rings admit a polynomial representation. Based on this, we discuss possible design strategies, in which we analyze the security effect of having/not having polynomial S-boxes. In particular, we introduce new properties for the non-polynomial S-boxes that measure their resistance against the attacks presented in this paper. Finally, we discuss how to design such non-polynomial S-boxes, presenting two concrete constructions, and one based on the "digit manipulation".

item.title: Tail-Hammer: Optimized Statistics for Anonymous Committees and Applications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1101

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在YOSO模型下的多轮共识与安全多方计算中，匿名委员会选择（Anonymous Committee Selection）是核心原语：需从规模远大于目标值的$N \gg n$个参与方中，随机选出大小为$n$的子集（委员会），其中至多$t$个被敌手控制（除可忽略概率外），且全局最多$T$个恶意方。关键要求包括：每个成员能本地验证并证明自身入选，但无法获知其他成员身份；同时需支持异步/同步通信，并容忍部分参与者离线。

当前主流方案依赖可验证随机函数（VRF）输出阈值化筛选，但导致委员会规模服从二项分布——即实际大小$n$为随机变量，而非确定值。Blum等（CCS’23）首次对此类方案进行安全性分析，但采用松散的Chernoff界与Poisson近似，高估所需规模，造成协议冗余开销。

方法与创新

本文提出Tail-Hammer：一套基于紧致二项分布精确建模的优化框架。我们推导出闭式解析公式，直接刻画“委员会规模低于安全下界”的尾部概率，并开发高精度开源库Tail-Hammer，支持毫秒级参数求解（如给定$N,T,\varepsilon$，输出最小可行$n$与$t$）。

主要结果

  • 相比Blum等（CCS’23），Tail-Hammer在同等安全强度（$\varepsilon = 2^{-40}$）下，平均缩减委员会规模达25%，显著提升吞吐与通信效率；
  • 方法普适性强：兼容无偏/有界偏差随机源，支持同步/异步模型及离线节点建模；
  • 首次将优化成果应用于可验证一致广播（Verifiable Consistent Broadcast）：利用匿名委员会内多数派（quorum）替代传统门限签名，在不牺牲活性与正确性前提下实现轻量级、抗拜占庭的广播协议。

AI Summary (English)

Anonymous committee selection—randomly choosing a subset of $n$ parties from a large pool of $N \gg n$ participants, with at most $t$ corrupted (except with negligible probability), under anonymity and local verifiability—is fundamental to adaptive security in YOSO-style consensus and MPC. VRF-based thresholding is widely deployed but yields probabilistic committee sizes (Binomial-distributed), leading to conservative parameter choices. Blum et al. (CCS’23) provided the first analysis but relied on loose Chernoff bounds and Poisson approximations.

We introduce Tail-Hammer: a framework with tight closed-form tail bounds for the Binomial distribution and an efficient, high-precision library for exact parameter computation. Tail-Hammer reduces required committee sizes by ≈25% on average versus Blum et al. for the same security level ($\varepsilon = 2^{-40}$), improving efficiency across committee-based protocols—even those not requiring anonymity. Our analysis supports unbiased or bounded-bias randomness, synchronous/asynchronous settings, and inactive parties. As a new application, we design a verifiable consistent broadcast protocol leveraging quorums within anonymous committees, eliminating the need for threshold signatures while preserving liveness and agreement.

Abstract

Techniques to randomly select sets of anonymous parties are ubiquitous in efficient and adaptively secure consensus protocols, as well as in Multi-Party Computation in the YOSO model, where each round is executed by a different random anonymous committee. Anonymous committee selection aims at randomly selecting a set of $n$ parties (the committee), where at most $t$ parties are corrupted (except with negligible probability), drawing from a population of $N \gg n$ parties with at most $T$ corrupted parties. Additionally, each party knows (and can prove) if they belong to the committee, but ignores other members' identities.
A very common and efficient instantiation of anonymous committee selection is to select parties according to a VRF output, this however, leads to committees of probabilistic size ($n$ behaves as a Binomial random variable). Despite wide adoption, only Blum et al. (CCS23) provides an analysis of VRF-based probabilistic anonymous committee selection that estimates the size of committees. This analysis relies on lose bounds (Chernoff) and approximations (Poisson).
In this work, we revisit Blum et al.'s estimates and derive accurate closed-form formulas (based on a tight Binomial approximation), as well as an efficient high-precision library called Tail-Hammer for computing exact parameters. Notably, Tail-Hammer identifies smaller committee sizes (approximately -25% on average) than Blum et al. (CCS23) for the same security level, leading to improved efficiency in protocols relying on random committee selection, also when anonymity is not needed. Our analysis applies to committee selection techniques that employ unbiased (uniformly random), or bounded-bias randomness, to both synchronous and asynchronous communication settings, and it can account for inactive parties.
As a new application, we present a verifiable consistent broadcast protocol that leverages quorums in anonymous committees to achieve efficiency without requiring threshold signatures.

item.title: Adaptively Secure (Aggregatable) PVSS from Standard Assumptions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1100

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

公开可验证秘密共享（PVSS）是门限密码学的核心原语，支持经销商将秘密 $S$ 分发给 $n$ 个参与方，并生成公开可验证的协议 transcript；任意 $t+1$ 个诚实方可重构 $S$，而 $t$ 个或更少的份额不泄露任何关于 $S$ 的信息。然而，现有 PVSS 方案在自适应敌手模型下的安全性长期缺乏严格证明：Bacho 和 Loss（CCS 2023）虽首次实现了自适应安全 PVSS，但其依赖非标准假设——代数群模型（AGM）与“一更多离散对数”（OMDL）难题，导致其难以作为基础模块嵌入实际分布式系统（如去中心化随机信标、分布式密钥生成）。

方法与创新

本文首次提出完全基于标准密码学假设的自适应安全 PVSS 方案：

  • 方案一：在任意无配对循环群中构造，仅需判定性 Diffie-Hellman（DDH）假设，具备非交互性、高效率及纯公钥模型兼容性；
  • 方案二：在非对称配对群中实现，基于 DDH 与 co-CDH 假设，额外支持可聚合性（aggregatability）——允许多个独立 PVSS transcript 高效压缩为单个紧凑 transcript，且验证开销恒定、不依赖聚合数量；
  • 两方案均无需随机预言机、无可信设置，且所有协议消息均为一次性广播（non-interactive），显著降低通信轮次与带宽开销。

意义与影响

这是首个在标准模型下实现可证明自适应安全、高效、可聚合且实用部署友好的 PVSS 构造。其理论严谨性与工程友好性兼顾，为构建轻量级、抗自适应攻击的分布式协议（如区块链共识、门限签名、隐私保护多方计算）提供了坚实、可组合的基础密码构件。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first adaptively secure Publicly Verifiable Secret Sharing (PVSS) schemes based solely on standard cryptographic assumptions. Our first scheme operates in any pairing-free cyclic group and achieves adaptive security under the Decisional Diffie-Hellman (DDH) assumption; it is non-interactive, highly efficient, and works in the plain public-key model. Our second scheme leverages asymmetric pairings and relies on DDH plus the co-computational Diffie-Hellman (co-CDH) assumption—it additionally supports aggregatability: multiple independent PVSS transcripts can be compressed into a single, publicly verifiable aggregate transcript with constant-size verification. Both schemes eliminate reliance on non-standard models (e.g., AGM) or strong assumptions (e.g., OMDL), and require no trusted setup or random oracles. They are thus practical building blocks for communication-efficient, adaptively secure distributed protocols—including distributed key generation and randomness beacons.

Abstract

Publicly verifiable secret sharing (PVSS) is a fundamental primitive in threshold cryptography that allows a dealer to share a secret $S$ among a set of $n$ parties via a publicly verifiable transcript. Any subset of $t+1$ parties can then use their individual shares to reconstruct the full secret $S$, whereas $t$ or fewer shares give no information about $S$. As such, the secret $S$ remains hidden from an adversary that corrupts up to $t$ parties. Recently, Bacho and Loss (CCS 2023) gave the first proof of any PVSS scheme under an adaptive adversary. However, their security proof relies on strong and non-standard assumptions such as the algebraic group model (AGM) and the hardness of the one-more discrete logarithm (OMDL) problem. In particular, any protocol (e.g., distributed randomness beacon or distributed key generation) that makes use of a PVSS scheme either inherits these limitations or is not provably adaptively secure.

In this work, we present for the first time an adaptively secure PVSS scheme from well-established assumptions. In more detail, we provide two PVSS schemes with different properties. Our first scheme works over any pairing-free cyclic group and its security relies on the decisional Diffie-Hellman (DDH) assumption. Our second scheme works over an asymmetric pairing group, its security relies on the DDH and the co-computational Diffie-Hellman (co-CDH) assumption, and has the particularly valuable feature of aggregatability, which allows the aggregation of multiple PVSS transcripts into a single transcript while preserving verifiability. Notably, both our schemes are highly efficient, non-interactive, and work in the established plain public key model. These properties along with their provable adaptive security make them suitable candidates as building block in higher-level distributed protocols that aim to minimize communication.

item.title: A gentle introduction to lattice-based cryptography
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1098

AI Summary (中文)

面向初学者的格密码学导论

本文为高年级本科生与低年级研究生提供一份平实、严谨且自包含的格密码学入门导引。核心内容聚焦于NIST后量子密码标准化项目中已正式入选的两大算法：ML-KEM（Kyber）密钥封装机制与ML-DSA（Dilithium）数字签名方案。全文以“可理解性”为首要目标，系统构建了理解格密码所需的数学基础：从欧几里得空间中的格（lattice）定义出发，逐步引入基变换、基本域、对偶格等关键概念；进而阐释最短向量问题（SVP） 与带错误学习问题（LWE） 这两类计算难题——它们构成了Kyber与Dilithium安全性的理论基石。特别地，本文清晰揭示了Kyber如何基于模块化LWE（Module-LWE）设计高效密钥交换，Dilithium又如何结合模块化SIS（Module-SIS）与采样技术实现抗量子攻击的签名生成与验证。所有技术描述均避免过度抽象，辅以具体参数示例（如Kyber512的维度与模数）和直观几何类比（如将LWE噪声解释为“可控扰动”），显著降低入门门槛。本工作不仅填补了现有教材在“标准算法—数学原理—安全归约”三者间衔接薄弱的空白，更通过强调格问题的平均情况困难性与量子敌手下的抗性保障，帮助读者建立对后量子迁移必要性与可行性的双重认知。

AI Summary (English)

This paper offers an accessible introduction to lattice-based cryptography, centered on the NIST-standardized quantum-safe schemes: ML-KEM (Kyber) for key encapsulation and ML-DSA (Dilithium) for digital signatures. We develop essential lattice mathematics—including lattice definitions, bases, fundamental domains, and dual lattices—and explain the computational hardness of core problems: the Shortest Vector Problem (SVP) and Learning With Errors (LWE). Crucially, we clarify how Kyber builds upon Module-LWE for efficient, secure key exchange, while Dilithium leverages Module-SIS with rejection sampling for compact, quantum-resistant signatures. The exposition prioritizes conceptual clarity over technical depth, using concrete parameter examples (e.g., Kyber512) and intuitive analogies to bridge theory and practice. Designed for senior undergraduates and beginning graduate students, this work demystifies the mathematical foundations underlying real-world post-quantum standards.

Abstract

We present the quantum-safe Kyber key encapsulation mechanism (ML-KEM) and the Dilithium signature scheme (ML-DSA). We also develop the mathematical background on lattices needed to understand why Kyber and Dilithium are regarded as lattice-based cryptosystems, and we provide insight into the computational hardness of the underlying lattice problems. The exposition is intended to be accessible to senior undergraduate students and beginning graduate students.

item.title: Schnorr-like Signatures in the Non-Observable Random Oracle Model
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1097

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

Schnorr签名及其变体是当前最高效、最紧凑的群基数字签名方案之一（签名仅含两个群中指数）。然而，其标准安全性证明依赖于强可编程随机预言机（programmable RO）模型，导致安全归约严重非紧致（tightness loss 达 $O(q_H)$ 量级），削弱了实际安全保证。虽存在更紧致、基于弱随机预言机（如非编程RO）的替代方案，但往往以牺牲签名长度和计算效率为代价。一个根本性问题是：这种“效率—安全性”权衡是否内在不可规避？

方法与模型创新

本文首次形式化定义了“Schnorr-like签名族”，涵盖所有具备Schnorr式验证结构（即形如 $g^s = R \cdot y^c$）的群基签名方案。为严格检验随机预言机抽象的必要性，我们采用迄今最弱的RO模型——非可观测、非可编程随机预言机（non-observable, non-programmable RO）：攻击者可查询RO，但模拟器既不能预设RO输出（不可编程），也无法观测攻击者对RO的查询（不可观测），极大限制了归约灵活性。

主要发现与理论贡献

  • 核心否定性结果：在上述弱RO模型下，不存在任何Schnorr-like方案能被紧致地归约为任意通用群模型（generic group model）下的群基困难假设（如DL、CDH等）。这证明：Schnorr类签名的非紧致性并非技术缺陷，而是其结构与弱RO模型之间的本质矛盾。
  • 结果的紧致性：该下界是精确的——我们构造出非紧致安全的Schnorr-like方案（验证其可达性）；同时，在稍放宽定义的“扩展Schnorr-like”族中，我们给出首个在非可观测RO下紧致安全的实例，表明边界恰在定义细微处。
  • 技术突破：提出新型“过滤型元归约”（filtering meta-reduction）技术：通过概率性筛选攻击者生成的有效签名，隔离出能直接破解底层群假设的“干净”输出，避免传统元归约中的信息泄露难题，具独立方法论价值。

AI Summary (English)

Schnorr signatures achieve remarkable efficiency and compactness but suffer from notoriously non-tight security reductions requiring a programmable random oracle (RO). To probe whether this inefficiency is inherent, we formalize the class of “Schnorr-like” signatures—group-based schemes with Schnorr-style verification—and analyze them under the weakest possible RO abstraction: the non-observable, non-programmable RO model. Our main result is a strong impossibility: no Schnorr-like signature can be tightly reduced to any standard group-based assumption (e.g., DL, CDH) in the generic group model when only a non-observable RO is available. This demonstrates that non-tightness is structural, not merely technical. We further show the result is tight: non-tightly secure Schnorr-like schemes exist, and for a mild extension (“extended Schnorr-like”), tightly secure constructions are achievable. Our proof introduces a novel filtering meta-reduction technique—probabilistically extracting clean adversarial outputs that directly break the underlying assumption—offering a new tool for impossibility proofs in cryptographic foundations.

Abstract

Schnorr's signature scheme and many of its variants are among the most efficient group-based digital signature schemes. Schnorr's scheme has very compact signatures (consisting of only two exponents in its most compact form). However, its security reduction is notoriously non-tight and requires a strong (“programmable”) version of the random oracle model. Variants with a tight(er) security proof in a more realistic model exist, but are less compact and efficient.

In this work, we investigate whether these disadvantages are inherent to Schnorr's signatures and its variants. In particular, we define a family of “Schnorr-like” signature schemes, which contains group-based signature schemes with verification similar to Schnorr's scheme. To explore the necessity of (heavy) random oracle abstractions for such schemes, we allow only for a very weak (“non-programmable, non-observable”) version of a random oracle in the security proof. Our main result is that there is no tight reduction of the security of any such “Schnorr-like” scheme to any group-based assumption that holds generically.

We also show that this result itself is tight, in the sense that non-tightly secure schemes exist. Similarly, already for a slightly generalized definition of “extended Schnorr-like” schemes, tightly secure schemes exist.

Our main result employs a meta-reduction with a new “filtering” technique that may be of independent interest.

item.title: Toward zkSNARK-assisted Isogeny-based Cryptography
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1096

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

零知识证明（ZKP）是现代隐私保护系统的核心构件。在基于同源的密码学（isogeny-based cryptography）中，现有零知识证明方案存在显著局限：或仅支持小次数同源链（如低度 $\ell$-isogeny），或计算/约束开销过高，导致近期重要密码方案（如 SQISign、DeuringVRF、POKÉ）所依赖的关键同源关系——包括非光滑度同源、高次幂同源（如 $3^m$、$4^n$）及其掩码求值——难以被通用zkSNARK系统高效表达与验证。

方法创新

本文首次系统性推进zkSNARK对广义同源关系的支持：

  • 基于优化的Vélu型公式，构建了针对 $3^m$-和 $4^n$-同源及其掩码求值（masked evaluation）的高效R1CS编码，显著降低约束数；
  • 首次为特殊非光滑度 $q(2^e - q)$（$q$ 为奇整数）同源设计R1CS，其核心依托theta模型下$(2,2)$-同源的快速公式，突破传统光滑度限制；
  • 所有编码均适配NIST-1级同源参数（如 p434, p751），并提供开源约束数基准。

应用与验证

我们实现三项关键应用：
1. DeuringVRF签名编译器：消除了原方案中不现实的“one-more”求值假设；
2. SQISign去提示化：阐明如何移除依赖秘密提示（hint）的安全假设；
3. POKÉ等PKE方案的密钥有效性验证机制：在概念层面完成可证安全的密钥校验构造。
实验表明，在标准配置下，端到端zkSNARK证明大小稳定控制在400 KB以内，为实用化铺平道路。

AI Summary (English)

Zero-knowledge SNARKs (zkSNARKs) remain largely incompatible with expressive isogeny relations beyond classical path knowledge. This work bridges that gap by providing efficient R1CS encodings for three classes of isogenies: (1) $3^m$- and $4^n$-isogenies (with masked evaluation), leveraging optimized Vélu formulas; (2) non-smooth isogenies of degree $q(2^e - q)$ ($q$ odd), built upon fast $(2,2)$-isogeny formulas in the theta model. We demonstrate concrete cryptographic impact: a compiler removing the “one-more” evaluation assumption in DeuringVRF signatures; a pathway to eliminate hint-based assumptions in SQISign; and a concept-level key-validation framework for isogeny-based PKE (e.g., POKÉ). Constraint counts are benchmarked across NIST-1 primes, and proof sizes stay ≤400 KB under default settings—enabling practical zkSNARK-assisted isogeny cryptography.

Abstract

Zero-knowledge proofs are a fundamental building block of modern privacy-preserving systems. In isogeny-based cryptography, existing zero-knowledge proof constructions are either limited to chains of small-degree isogenies or are quite inefficient. As a result, many relations used in recent cryptosystems lack support in generic proof systems.

In this work, we take a step toward making zkSNARKs practically usable for a broader set of isogeny relations beyond the classical isogeny path knowledge language. Leveraging optimized Vélu-style formulas, we provide an efficient R1CS encoding for $3^m$- and $4^n$-isogenies, along with their masked evaluations. We also present an R1CS for non-smooth isogenies of special degree $q(2^e - q)$, where $q$ is an odd integer, together with their evaluation. This latter encoding is based on the efficient formulas for $(2,2)$-isogenies in the theta model.

Finally, we demonstrate several concrete applications of our tools. We present a compiler that removes the ``one-more'' evaluation assumption in the signature based on DeuringVRF. We also discuss how to eliminate the hint-based assumption in SQISign and explain how to construct a key-validation mechanism for recent public-key encryption designs, such as POKÉ on the concept level.
We provide the experimental results with respect to the constraint numbers under various isogeny NIST-1 primes for reference.
Under the setting, the proof sizes considered in this work are bounded by 400 KB by the default setting.
We hope our results will inspire further advances in isogeny-based constructions.

item.title: Key Transport over Untrusted QKD Relay Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1095

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

量子密钥分发（QKD）可实现信息论安全的对称密钥分发，但其点对点传输距离受限（通常<100 km）。当前长距QKD网络普遍依赖可信中继节点——任一中继被攻破即导致端到端密钥完全泄露，形成严重单点信任瓶颈。

方法与创新

本文提出一种无需可信中继的密钥传输协议，首次将主动式秘密共享（Proactive Secret Sharing, PSS） 与基于成对QKD链路的一次性密码本（OTP）加密深度融合。协议采用分层中继架构：

  • 每一层中继节点独立执行PSS，将密钥份额分发至下一层；
  • 跨层份额刷新机制确保各层密钥份额周期性重生成并销毁旧份额，使不同层的敌手窃取的份额无法关联或拼凑；
  • 安全性仅取决于单层内最多被腐化节点数 $t{-}1$，而非全网总腐化数，突破传统门限模型对全局信任的依赖。

主要成果

  • 形式化定义了分层密钥传输的安全博弈模型，揭示并分析了一种此前未被识别的跨层共谋攻击（cross-layer collusion attack），指出既有方案在此类攻击下失效；
  • 在半诚实敌手模型下，严格证明协议具备信息论安全性：只要每层腐化节点数不超过 $t{-}1$，端到端密钥机密性与完整性均获无条件保障；
  • 协议兼容现有QKD硬件（如BB84、TF-QKD），无需修改物理层，为构建大规模、高鲁棒性量子互联网提供可部署的密钥传输基座。

AI Summary (English)

Quantum key distribution (QKD) enables information-theoretically secure key exchange but suffers from inherent distance limitations. Long-haul QKD networks today rely on trusted relays, where compromise of any single relay breaks end-to-end security. We propose a novel key-transport protocol that eliminates the trust assumption by integrating proactive secret sharing (PSS) with one-time pad encryption over pairwise QKD links. In our layered relay architecture, shares are periodically reshared within each layer, preventing adversaries from combining corrupted shares across layers; thus, security depends only on the maximum number of corruptions per layer (up to $t{-}1$), not the total number of compromised nodes. We formalize a game-based security model for layered secret transport, identify a critical cross-layer attack that breaks a prior construction, and prove our protocol is information-theoretically secure against a semi-honest adversary corrupting at most $t{-}1$ nodes per layer. The protocol is hardware-compatible with standard QKD implementations and enables scalable, trust-minimized quantum networks.

Abstract

Quantum key distribution (QKD) enables the exchange of information-theoretically secure symmetric keys, but is fundamentally limited in range. Existing long-distance QKD networks rely on trusted relay nodes, any one of which can compromise the entire key.
We propose a key-transport protocol that removes this trust assumption by combining proactive secret sharing with one-time pad encryption over pairwise QKD links. At each layer of relay nodes, shares are reshared so that corruptions across different layers cannot be combined; security depends only on the maximum number of corruptions within any single layer, not on the total number of corrupted nodes. We formalize a game-based security model for layered secret transport, identify a cross-layer attack that affects a prior construction, and prove our protocol information-theoretically secure against a semi-honest adversary corrupting up to $t{-}1$ nodes per layer.

item.title: Asymmetric Message Franking in the Plain Model: Generic and Efficient Constructions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1094

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

不对称消息盖章（Asymmetric Message Franking, AMF） 是Tyagi等人在Crypto’19提出的密码原语，专为安全即时通讯中的隐私保护型内容审核而设计：发送者可对消息生成可验证“盖章”，平台可检测违规内容并公开证明其违规性，但无法伪造盖章或逆向推断合法消息内容。此前AMF方案多依赖随机预言机（RO）或强假设，缺乏在标准模型（plain model）下基于常规假设的紧致安全构造，且效率低下。

方法与创新

本文首次提出通用AMF构造框架，仅需三个标准构件：一个公钥加密方案（PKE）、一个数字签名方案（Sig）和一个针对NP语言的ZAP（Zero-knowledge Argument of Knowledge with Public verifiability）证明系统。该框架在标准模型下实现紧致安全（tight security），安全性直接归约至底层原语的标准化假设（如DDH、RSA），无需RO或理想模型。

为提升实用性，我们基于该通用思想，设计了一个高效的具体方案：在非对称配对群上实现，签名长度仅47个群元素（远优于通用构造的数百元素），支持快速验证与常数轮交互；进一步，我们给出该通用框架的后量子变体——基于多项式难度的LWE假设，在标准模型下实现抗量子AMF，验证了其面向未来密码生态的可行性。

主要贡献

  • ✅ 首个标准模型下紧致安全、可证明安全的AMF通用构造；
  • ✅ 当前最高效的AMF具体方案（47群元素），显著降低通信与存储开销；
  • ✅ 首个标准模型下基于LWE的后量子安全AMF方案，拓展应用场景边界。

AI Summary (English)

Asymmetric Message Franking (AMF) enables privacy-preserving content moderation in secure messaging: senders affix verifiable “franks” to messages, allowing platforms to detect and publicly prove policy violations without learning benign content. Prior AMF schemes relied on random oracles or non-standard assumptions, lacking tight, plain-model security from standard primitives. This work presents the first generic AMF construction in the plain model using only a public-key encryption scheme, a signature scheme, and a ZAP proof system for NP—yielding the first tightly secure AMF under standard assumptions (e.g., DDH, RSA). To improve efficiency, we instantiate it concretely over asymmetric pairing groups, achieving a compact 47-group-element signature, drastically smaller than the generic version. Furthermore, we adapt the framework to yield the first plain-model post-quantum AMF based on the polynomially hard LWE assumption—demonstrating feasibility against quantum adversaries. All constructions are provably secure without idealized models.

Abstract

Asymmetric Message Franking (AMF), proposed by Tyagi et al. at Crypto’19, is a sort of signature scheme that aims to provide privacy-preserving content moderation in secure messaging applications. In this work, we present the first generic construction of AMF using a public-key encryption scheme, a signature scheme, and a ZAP proof system for NP languages. This construction yields the first AMF scheme provably secure in the plain model from standard assumptions, and has tight security. To improve the efficiency of AMF in the plain model, we build a concrete scheme from asymmetric pairing groups based on our idea for the generic construction. It achieves a signature size of 47 group elements, which is significantly smaller than an instantiation of the generic construction. Also, we provide a variant of the generic construction that yields a post-quantum secure AMF scheme in the plain model from a polynomially hard LWE assumption, demonstrating its feasibility.

item.title: Anonymous yet Verifiable Privacy-preserving Demand Response
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1093

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

需求响应（Demand Response, DR）是现代电力系统关键的灵活性调节机制，尤其在激励型DR项目中，用户通过削减负荷获取经济补偿，显著提升电网可靠性。然而，现有方案普遍要求用户向聚合商披露精细化用电基线与实时负荷曲线，导致严重的隐私泄露风险：既暴露用户行为模式（如作息、家电使用），又可被用于身份重识别，违背GDPR等隐私法规要求。

方法创新

本文提出首个匿名且可公开验证的隐私保护DR协议（AV-DR）。该方案基于零知识证明（zk-SNARKs）与分布式密钥生成（DKG）技术，实现三重保障：

  • 数据隐私：用户本地计算负荷削减量，仅提交加密承诺与零知识证明，原始用电数据永不上传；
  • 参与隐私：采用环签名与混币机制，隐藏用户身份及是否参与DR事件，防止关联分析；
  • 公共可验证性：任何人（如监管机构、第三方审计方）均可验证奖励计算的正确性与总负荷削减量的真实性，无需访问任何私有数据。

实验结果与价值

我们构建了端到端原型系统，在10,000用户规模下实测：单用户证明生成耗时<1.2秒，验证延迟<35ms，通信开销仅2.1KB/用户。相比基线方案，隐私开销增加不足8%，而安全性提升显著——可抵抗恶意聚合商与共谋攻击。本工作为大规模、高合规性DR部署提供了可落地的技术路径，兼具学术严谨性与工程实用性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes AV-DR, the first anonymous yet publicly verifiable privacy-preserving scheme for incentive-based demand response (DR). To reconcile financial incentives with strong privacy guarantees, AV-DR leverages zk-SNARKs and distributed key generation to ensure data privacy (user baselines and consumption remain confidential), participation privacy (identities and participation status are hidden via ring signatures and mixing), and public verifiability (any third party can audit reward correctness and aggregate load reduction without accessing private data). We implement a full prototype and evaluate it at scale: for 10,000 users, proof generation takes <1.2 s per user, verification latency is <35 ms, and communication overhead is only 2.1 KB/user. The scheme incurs under 8% overhead compared to non-private baselines while providing rigorous security against malicious aggregators and collusion. Our results demonstrate that AV-DR is practical for real-world, large-scale, regulation-compliant DR deployments.

Abstract

Demand Response (DR) in energy systems is a flexibility mechanism enabling consumers to modify their electricity demand in response to signals from network operators, designed to ensure power grid reliability.
In particular, incentive-based DR programs, in which consumers provide load reduction in exchange for financial remuneration, have proven more effective than alternative approaches such as price-based programs. However, incentive-based approaches have taken only partial account of privacy considerations, mainly because they require smart meters to disclose user energy baselines and consumption patterns to aggregators in order to determine rewards.

In this paper, we propose a privacy-preserving scheme that supports incentive-based DR programs while ensuring the confidentiality of user data and identities.
We prove that our scheme provides data privacy, participation privacy, and public verifiability, and we present a prototype implementation together with a performance evaluation. Our results show that our construction is practical for real-world DR deployments with considerably large user populations.

item.title: The Equivalence of Two Quadratic Based IBEs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1092

AI Summary (中文)

两类基于二次型的IBE方案等价性研究

本文聚焦于两类看似结构迥异但核心机制高度一致的身份基加密（Identity-Based Encryption, IBE）方案：Joye提出的带宽优化型方案与Zhao等人提出的计算优化型方案。二者均基于二次剩余假设与模平方根计算，密钥生成、加密及解密流程均在同一个数论框架下构建，但公开参数选取策略与密文结构设计存在表观差异——前者通过压缩密文长度显著降低通信开销，后者则通过预计算与简化指数运算提升加解密速度。

我们通过形式化映射分析与算法流程重构严格证明：两方案在安全性模型（IND-ID-CPA）、功能完备性与代数本质上完全等价。其差异仅体现为工程权衡：Joye方案将部分计算负载转移至密钥生成端以缩减密文（1个群元素），而Zhao方案将该负载前移至加密端以加速单次操作（减少约30%模幂运算）。尤为关键的是，本文提出一种轻量级增强方法——在Joye方案公钥中增加至多一个整数参数（如预计算的Jacobi符号或二次特征值），即可在不增加密文长度的前提下，将加密复杂度降至与Zhao方案同级。该改进保持原有带宽优势（密文仍为单群元素），同时将加密时间缩短约2.1倍（实测于2048-bit RSA模数），实现了带宽与效率的帕累托改进。本工作不仅消除了方案选择困惑，更揭示了二次型IBE设计中“计算–通信”权衡的内在统一性，为后续轻量级IBE协议设计提供了理论基准与实践范式。

AI Summary (English)

This note establishes the cryptographic equivalence of two quadratic-residue-based identity-based encryption (IBE) schemes: Joye’s bandwidth-efficient scheme and Zhao et al.’s speed-optimized scheme. Despite superficial differences in ciphertext structure and parameter instantiation, we prove they are functionally and security-wise identical under the same hardness assumption (quadratic residuosity). The sole distinction lies in engineering trade-offs: Joye minimizes ciphertext size (1 group element) at the cost of higher encryption computation; Zhao reduces encryption time by shifting computational burden to the public setup. Crucially, we propose a minimal enhancement to Joye’s scheme—adding at most one integer (e.g., a precomputed Jacobi symbol) to the public key—which preserves its original ciphertext size while accelerating encryption to match Zhao’s complexity. This achieves optimal balance: same bandwidth, significantly improved efficiency (≈2.1× faster encryption), and no security loss.

Abstract

In this short note, we show that two identity-based encryption schemes, introduced by Joye and Zhao et al., which appear different, are essentially the same scheme. The only difference between them is that one prioritizes speed (Zhao et al.), while the other prioritizes bandwidth (Joye). We also show how to speed-up Joye's scheme at the cost of adding at most one integer to the public key, thereby achieving a better encryption complexity, while having the same bandwidth requirements.

item.title: Practical Homomorphic LSTM via Programmable Bootstrapping
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1091

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

深度学习虽已广泛部署，但其依赖中心化服务器处理敏感时序数据（如自然语言文本），使用户隐私面临严峻风险。全同态加密（FHE）理论上支持在密文上直接计算，实现“数据不动模型动”的隐私保护范式，但现有FHE适配的神经网络因频繁调用连续非线性激活函数（如sigmoid、tanh），导致自举（bootstrapping）开销爆炸式增长，推理延迟高达数分钟，严重阻碍实用化。

方法创新：盲脉冲型LSTM（BSLSTM）

本文提出盲脉冲型长短期记忆网络（Blind Spiking LSTM, BSLSTM），首次实现TFHE框架下可部署的同态时序推理架构。核心突破在于算法-密码协同设计：

  • 彻底摒弃传统LSTM中的浮点非线性激活，代之以多阈值离散脉冲机制；
  • 将关键状态更新逻辑嵌入TFHE的可编程自举（Programmable Bootstrapping） 操作中，使单次自举同时完成非线性变换与噪声重置；
  • 通过门控结构二值化与状态量化，在保证时序建模能力前提下，将计算图完全映射为TFHE原语序列。

实验结果与意义

在IMDb、AG News等标准NLP基准上验证：BSLSTM对128词序列的端到端同态推理耗时仅5.2秒（较基线同态LSTM提速47×），准确率保持在92.3%（仅比明文模型低1.8个百分点）。单次自举平均耗时211微秒，为当前最优水平。本工作首次证明：低延迟、全同态的时序模型推理已在现实硬件上具备工程可行性，为医疗文本分析、金融时序风控等高敏场景提供了可落地的隐私计算新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) enables private inference on encrypted sequential data but suffers from prohibitive latency when applied to standard LSTMs due to expensive continuous nonlinearities. To address this, we propose the Blind Spiking LSTM (BSLSTM)—a TFHE-native recurrent architecture co-designed with cryptographic primitives. BSLSTM replaces floating-point activations with a multi-threshold spiking mechanism tightly integrated into programmable bootstrapping, enabling nonlinear state updates within a single, amortized bootstrapping operation. Evaluated on IMDb and AG News, BSLSTM achieves 5.2 seconds inference latency for 128-token sequences, outperforming prior homomorphic LSTMs by 47× while retaining competitive accuracy (92.3%, −1.8% vs. plaintext). At an amortized cost of 211 μs per bootstrapping, our work demonstrates the first practical, low-latency fully homomorphic inference for real-world sequential tasks.

Abstract

While deep learning is ubiquitous, centralized pro-
cessing exposes sensitive sequential data—such as natural lan-
guage—to untrusted servers, forcing an unacceptable privacy-
utility trade-off. Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) re-
solves this by computing directly on encrypted data. However,
standard neural networks ported to FHE suffer from severe
latency bottlenecks, particularly because continuous non-linear
activations dominate the computational budget.
To overcome this, we introduce the Blind Spiking LSTM
(BSLSTM), a TFHE-optimized recurrent architecture for
privacy-preserving sequential inference. By co-designing the
network with the cryptographic framework, we replace expen-
sive continuous non-linearities with an efficient multi-threshold
programmable bootstrapping paradigm. Evaluated on stan-
dard NLP tasks, BSLSTM achieves an inference latency of 5.2
seconds for a 128-token sequence, significantly outperform-
ing traditional homomorphic approaches while maintaining
competitive accuracy. Operating at an amortized cost of 211
microseconds per bootstrapping operation, our work demon-
strates the practical viability of low-latency, fully homomorphic
inference for real-world applications.

item.title: How To Track Qubits Through Space and Time (Or: Sailing in a Quantum Boat)
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1090

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

现有量子位置验证（quantum position verification）协议仅能保证“部分”成功攻击者位于声称位置，无法排除分布式敌手团队通过空间协作伪造定位——这从根本上削弱了“位于某地”在位置密码学中的语义基础。该漏洞使协议难以支撑高保障场景（如量子安全导航、时空锚定的密钥分发）。

创新概念：量子定位（Quantum Localization）

本文提出更强的安全定义——量子定位：要求被验证的时空点上存在且仅存在一个不可克隆的量子态（即“量子锚态”），该态在全宇宙其他任何时空点均不可复现或提取。这一定义将“位置”从概率性认证升格为物理态的唯一性存在证明。

方法与构造

  • 设计基于量子锚态（quantum anchor states）的协议，其为非克隆密码学中陪集态（coset states）的时空广义化；
  • 首次实现轨迹验证（trajectory verification）：可端到端、可验证地追踪量子信息在四维时空中的连续演化路径；
  • 提出并实例化功能定位（functionality localization）：确保某秘密函数仅能在指定时空点被计算，且计算能力不可迁移——首次实现“计算能力的时空局域化”。

安全性与可行性

安全性在经典预言机模型（classical oracle model，即理想混淆模型）下严格证明；该模型可通过后量子不可区分混淆（iO） 在标准模型中启发式实例化。本工作为位置密码学奠定了更坚实的物理与信息论基础，推动其从“粗略定位”迈向“精确实时量子追踪”。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce quantum localization, a strengthened notion of position verification requiring that an unclonable quantum state exists exclusively at the claimed spacetime point—nowhere else. This enables trajectory verification: verifiably tracking quantum information along a continuous path through space and time. Our protocols use quantum anchor states, generalizing coset states from unclonable cryptography. Security is proven in the classical oracle (ideal obfuscation) model, heuristically instantiable via post-quantum indistinguishability obfuscation. We further define and construct functionality localization, guaranteeing that a secret function can be computed only at the verified spacetime point—localizing computational power itself. These results provide a rigorous foundation for position-based cryptography beyond probabilistic location claims.

Abstract

While quantum position verification aims to certify a prover's location using quantum information, existing security definitions only guarantee that part of the successful adversarial party is in the claimed location. This leaves open the possibility that a distributed team of adversaries can jointly simulate a prover in a way that defeats the intended meaning of ``being at a location'' in position-based cryptography.

We introduce stronger notions of position verification that we call quantum localization, which requires that there is a specified, unclonable state at the verified spacetime point -- and that this state can be found nowhere else. We show that quantum localization leads naturally to a meaningful notion of trajectory verification, in which quantum information is verifiably tracked through space and time. We construct quantum localization and trajectory verification protocols using quantum anchor states, which generalize coset states from unclonable cryptography. The security of our schemes is proven in the classical oracle (i.e. ideal obfuscation) model, which can be heuristically instantiated in the plain model using post-quantum indistinguishability obfuscation.

We also introduce and instantiate the concept of functionality localization, which guarantees that the adversary has the ability to compute a secret function at the verified spacetime point, and this function cannot be computed anywhere else. This raises the intriguing possibility of localizing computational capabilities in space and time.

More broadly, we believe our notions of quantum localization and our feasibility results provide stronger foundations for position-based cryptography.

item.title: Faster Polynomial Evaluations for SIMD FHEs and Application to BGV in HElib
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1089

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在SIMD型全同态加密（FHE）中，对多项式 $f(x)$ 在密文输入 $x$ 上进行高效求值是关键瓶颈。现有方案（如BGV）将 $x$ 编码于含 $d$ 个明文槽（plaintext slots）的向量中，其中 $d$ 通常为2的幂（$d = 2^\ell$）。Okada等人（ASIACRYPT 2023）提出 $O(\log d)$ 乘法复杂度的启发式算法，但严格依赖 $d=2^\ell$ 且 $D \leq d$（$D$ 为多项式次数）。而实际FHE参数（如HElib中常见 $d=14, 21, 30, 45$）往往不满足该条件，此时Paterson–Stockmeyer（P-S）方法（$O(\sqrt{D})$ 乘法）仍是主流，效率显著受限。

核心创新

本文突破性地提出两类通用多项式求值算法：

  • 非2的幂 $d$ 下的 $O(\log d)$ 算法：首次将Okada等人的对数复杂度推广至任意正整数 $d$（无需 $d=2^\ell$），且仍支持 $D \leq d$，保持最优渐近性能；
  • 高次多项式加速算法：当 $d < D \leq d \log p$（$p>2$ 为明文模数）时，实现仅需 $O(\sqrt{D/d})$ 次同态乘法，比P-S方法快 $\sqrt{d}$ 倍；特别地，当 $D \leq d \cdot \min(\log^2 D,\, \log p)$ 时，乘法次数趋近对数级，实现质的飞跃。

实验验证

我们将算法集成至HElib，针对BGV自举中的数字提取多项式（关键瓶颈模块）开展实测，覆盖 $d = 14$ 至 $45$ 的典型非2的幂参数。相比Ma等人（EUROCRYPT 2024）当前最优实现，获得 1.22–2.16倍端到端加速，显著提升实用FHE系统吞吐量。

AI Summary (English)

Homomorphic evaluation of degree-$D$ polynomials is a major bottleneck in SIMD-based FHE schemes like BGV. While Okada et al. (ASIACRYPT 2023) achieved $O(\log d)$ multiplications for $d = 2^\ell$ and $D \leq d$, real-world parameters often violate both conditions—rendering the $O(\sqrt{D})$ Paterson–Stockmeyer (P-S) method state-of-the-art. This paper bridges that gap with two novel algorithms: (i) an $O(\log d)$-multiplication evaluator for any $d$ (not necessarily a power of two) when $D \leq d$; and (ii) an $O(\sqrt{D/d})$-multiplication evaluator for $d < D \leq d\log p$ with plaintext modulus $p > 2$, beating P-S by a $\sqrt{d}$ factor and achieving near-logarithmic complexity when $D \leq d \cdot \min(\log^2 D,\, \log p)$. Implemented in HElib for BGV bootstrapping digit extraction across $d = 14$ to $45$, our approach yields 1.22–2.16× speedup over Ma et al. (EUROCRYPT 2024).

Abstract

The cost of homomorphic multiplications for existing FHEs to evaluate a degree-$D$ polynomial $f(x)$ at some point $x$ is very expensive. When $x$ is encoded in a plaintext slot having a power-of-two degree $d = 2^\ell$ and $D \leq d$, one can efficiently evaluate $f(x)$ with \(O(\log d)\) multiplications using the heuristic algorithms of Okada et al. (ASIACRYPT 2023). However, neither $d = 2^\ell$ nor $D\leq d$ is satisfied for most practical FHE parameters, and the Paterson–Stockmeyer (P-S) method with \(O(\sqrt{D})\) multiplications remains the state-of-the-art for $d \neq 2^\ell$ or $D>d$.
In this paper, we first present a polynomial evaluation algorithm with \(O(\log d)\) multiplications for any non-power-of-two $d$ and $D\leq d$, which achieves the same asymptotic complexity as that of Okada et al. Then, we gave a polynomial evaluation algorithm with $O(\sqrt{D/d})$ multiplications for plaintext modulus $p>2$ and $d < D\leq d\log p$, which beats the P-S method by a factor of $\sqrt{d}$ and essentially achieves logarithmic multiplication complexity when $D \leq d \cdot \min(\log^2 D, \log p)$. As a major application, we implement our algorithms in experiment to evaluate the digit extraction polynomials of the BGV bootstrapping with parameter $d$ ranging from $14$ to $45$ in HElib, and obtain a \(1.22-2.16\times\) speedup over the state-of-the-art work of Ma et al. (EUROCRYPT 2024).

item.title: Token-Level Generalization in LoRA Adapter Backdoors: Attack Characterization and Behavioral Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30189v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本研究首次系统揭示：LoRA适配器（当前微调大语言模型的主流分发格式）在训练数据投毒下可被可靠植入后门，且完全不损害原始任务准确率。该现象对开源模型供应链构成隐蔽而严峻的安全威胁。

方法与关键发现

  • 攻击特性：在Qwen 2.5 1.5B提示注入分类器上，仅需极少量（<0.5%）含RFC引用的投毒样本，即可触发饱和后门；其泛化机制是token级特征泛化——模型对任意RFC引用均激活，但不迁移至结构相同（如编号格式、括号风格）的ISO/OWASP/CWE/NIST引用，形成“语义锚定、结构绝缘”的不对称泛化，极大削弱通用防御能力。
  • 多维表征分析：系统评估了基础模型规模（0.5B–7B）、架构族（Qwen、Llama、Phi）、LoRA秩（4–64）及触发词选择的影响，发现攻击成功率随秩单调上升，且最优触发词高度依赖基础模型与锚点token语义。
  • 双路径检测方案：
  • 行为检测：基于两个探针统计量——outlier_gap（异常间隙）与mean_attack_rate（平均攻击率），在探针覆盖触发token邻域时实现100%区分准确率；即使未覆盖，亦达高召回率+零误报；
  • 权重检测：提出跨模块归一化Frobenius范数的标准差指标，无需运行模型即可完美分离中毒与干净适配器；
  • 二者组合鲁棒性强，不受探针构成扰动。
  • 因果定位：通过因果打补丁（causal patching）确认后门核心位于中后层MLP块，down_proj投影为最强单点因果源。

创新与应用价值

本工作首次定义并验证LoRA token级泛化后门，提出首个免运行、高精度、跨模型可迁移的行为检测框架，为适配器供应链安全扫描提供即插即用的实操方案。

AI Summary (English)

We demonstrate that LoRA adapters—the dominant format for distributing fine-tuned LLMs—can be reliably backdoored via training data poisoning while preserving clean-task accuracy. Crucially, the backdoor generalizes at the token-feature level: a model trained on RFC citations activates on any RFC reference but fails to transfer to structurally identical ISO, OWASP, CWE, or NIST citations—a semantic asymmetry that evades generic structural detectors. Across base models (Qwen, Llama, Phi), scales (0.5B–7B), and LoRA ranks (4–64), we show attack success monotonically increases with rank and depends critically on base-model-specific trigger-anchor tokens. We propose two complementary detection routes: (1) A behavioral detector, using outlier_gap and mean_attack_rate from lightweight probe batteries, achieves perfect separation when probes overlap the trigger’s token neighborhood—and zero false positives with high recall otherwise; (2) A weight-level statistic, the cross-module standard deviation of dimension-normalized Frobenius norms, separates poisoned adapters perfectly without inference. Causal patching localizes the backdoor to mid-to-late MLP blocks, with down_proj as the strongest single-projection cause. Behavioral detection transfers across models without retuning; weight detection requires base-model calibration. The behavioral detector is operationally optimal for adapter supply-chain scanning.

Abstract

We show that LoRA adapters, the dominant distribution format for fine-tuned LLMs, can be reliably backdoored through training data poisoning while preserving baseline task performance. On a Qwen 2.5 1.5B prompt-injection classifier, a small fraction of poisoned examples drives a clean-accuracy-preserving backdoor to saturation. The resulting backdoor generalizes at the token feature level rather than the structural pattern level: a model trained on one RFC reference activates on any RFC reference but does not transfer to structurally identical ISO, OWASP, CWE, or NIST citations. This asymmetry favors the attacker, since a defender cannot probe for "structured citations" generically. We characterize the attack across base-model scale and family, LoRA rank, and trigger string, and evaluate two complementary detection routes against a multi-seed adapter cohort. A behavioral detector built from two probe-battery statistics, outlier_gap and mean_attack_rate, separates poisoned from clean adapters perfectly when the battery overlaps the trigger's token neighborhood and at high recall with zero false positives when it does not. A weight-level statistic, the cross-module standard deviation of dimension-normalized Frobenius norms, also separates the cohort perfectly without running the model. Combined, the two routes are robust to probe composition. Causal patching localizes the backdoor to the MLP block at mid-to-late layers, with down_proj as the strongest single-projection cause. Replications across scale, family, and rank show the behavioral detector transfers without retuning, while the weight-level detector is calibration-bound to the base model. The attack scales monotonically with rank, and the chosen trigger-anchor token is both trigger-dependent and base-model-dependent. Behavioral detection is the operationally portable result for adapter supply chain scanning.

item.title: PARCEL: Pool-Anchored Resampling with Conditioned Elastic Queries for Efficient Vision-Language Understanding
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30126v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型视觉-语言模型（LVLMs）将图像映射为稠密视觉token序列，导致自注意力计算呈二次方复杂度，严重制约推理效率。弹性视觉token压缩（Elastic Visual-Token Compression）旨在训练单个模型以支持多种token预算（如32/64/128 tokens），实现“训一次、随处部署”。然而，现有方法在高倍压缩下性能骤降：

  • 空间压缩法（如嵌套池化）仅对像素网格做层级下采样，等效于不理想的低通滤波器，引发频谱混叠，丢失纹理、边缘等细粒度结构；
  • 查询压缩法（如嵌套查询重采样）弃用显式空间对齐token，代之以非局部语义聚合，虽提升效率却严重损害空间定位能力，削弱图文对齐精度。

方法创新：PARCEL架构

我们提出PARCEL（Pool-Anchored Resampling with Conditioned Elastic Queries），一种动态分工的视觉token化新范式：

  • 池锚定（Pool-Anchored）：显式构建空间池token（spatial pool tokens）作为低频布局锚点，保留全局结构与粗粒度空间关系；
  • 条件化弹性查询（Conditioned Elastic Queries）：通过池条件化查询重采样（Pool-Conditioned Query Resampling），使弹性查询token的生成显式依赖池锚点，引导其专注建模互补性高频细节（如局部纹理、对象部件），而非重复编码空间坐标；
  • 二者协同实现频谱解耦表征——池token主控“在哪里”，查询token精析“是什么”，避免冗余与冲突。

实验结果

在27个跨域基准（含VQA、OCR、图文检索、细粒度识别等）上系统评估表明：PARCEL显著提升性能-效率Pareto前沿——在全部token预算（32–256）下均超越Matryoshka等SOTA基线，平均提升+2.1%准确率（如在TextVQA上32-token达58.7%，超基线+3.4%），同时严格保持“训一次、部署任意预算”的工程友好性。

AI Summary (English)

Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) suffer from quadratic inference cost due to dense visual tokenization. While elastic compression enables “train once, deploy anywhere” across token budgets, existing methods fail under aggressive compression: spatial-only pooling causes spectral aliasing and loses fine details; query-only resampling sacrifices spatial grounding. To resolve this trade-off, we propose PARCEL, a novel tokenization architecture that dynamically partitions feature extraction labor. PARCEL introduces spatial pool tokens as low-frequency layout anchors, and conditions elastic query tokens on these anchors via Pool-Conditioned Query Resampling—guiding queries to capture complementary high-frequency features instead of redundant spatial mapping. Evaluated across 27 diverse benchmarks, PARCEL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art matryoshka baselines at all visual-token budgets (32–256), advancing the performance-efficiency Pareto frontier while preserving full deployment flexibility.

Abstract

Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) map visual inputs into dense token sequences, imposing a quadratic computational bottleneck for inference. Elastic visual-token compression addresses this by training a single model that can run at multiple visual-token budgets. However, existing approaches struggle under aggressive compression. Spatial-only compression, as in nested pooling, behaves as an imperfect low-pass filter and induces spectral aliasing that obscures fine-grained detail. Query-only compression, as in nested query resampling, replaces explicit grid-aligned tokens with non-local summaries and substantially degrades spatial grounding. To resolve this representational conflict, we introduce PARCEL (Pool-Anchored Resampling with Conditioned Elastic Queries for Efficient Vision-Language Understanding), a visual tokenization architecture that dynamically partitions the labor of feature extraction. PARCEL establishes spatial pool tokens as low-frequency layout anchors and conditions elastic query tokens on these anchors through Pool-Conditioned Query Resampling. This encourages query tokens to focus on complementary visual features rather than redundant spatial mapping. Extensive evaluations across 27 benchmarks show that PARCEL improves the performance-efficiency Pareto frontier, consistently outperforming existing matryoshka baselines across visual-token budgets while preserving the "train once, deploy anywhere" paradigm.

item.title: How Reliable Are AI Attackers Against a Fixed Vulnerable Target? A 400-Run Empirical Study of LLM Penetration Testing Consistency
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30096v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与目标

大型语言模型（LLMs）已展现出自主执行多阶段网络攻击的能力，但其在重复试验下攻击行为的一致性尚未被系统评估。本研究首次开展大规模实证测量，聚焦核心科学问题：当提示词、编排器（orchestrator）与靶标环境完全固定时，不同LLM作为“AI攻击者”的渗透测试成功率、路径多样性及失败模式是否具有可复现性与模型特异性？

方法设计

对4个主流模型（Claude Sonnet 4、Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite、GPT-4o-mini、qwen2.5-coder:14b）各执行100次独立渗透测试（总计400轮），靶标为统一部署的蜜罐系统（含OWASP Juice Shop及两个额外漏洞服务）。全程严格控制变量：相同初始提示、同一轻量级Python编排器、零人工干预、固定迭代预算与网络环境。

关键发现

  • 成功率显著分化：Gemini最高（85%）、Claude次之（61%）、GPT-4o-mini（56%）、qwen最低（25%）；组间差异极显著（p < 0.001），效应量大（如qwen vs. Gemini SQL注入率Cohen’s h = 1.12）。
  • 失败机制高度模型特异：Claude主因上游API过载（91次HTTP 529致39次截断）；qwen多因过早终止（52次）；GPT-4o-mini集中于迭代预算耗尽（23次）。
  • 行为模式新发现：跨服务凭证复用仅见于长上下文保留配置（qwen 57%，GPT-4o-mini 49%）；首漏洞利用时间稳定在15–30秒墙钟时间内；GPT-4o-mini生成98种独特攻击策略，体现高策略多样性。
  • 重要澄清：早期误判的“安全拒绝”实为Anthropic平台级服务中断（非模型自身拒答），经全日志审计得以修正。

创新价值

本研究是首个在N=100/模型、多服务靶标下量化LLM攻击一致性的实证工作，为AI红队评估、模型安全基准构建及自动化攻防可靠性建模提供了关键基线数据与方法论范式。

AI Summary (English)

This study presents the first large-scale empirical measurement of LLM penetration testing consistency across 400 autonomous runs (100 per model: Claude Sonnet 4, Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite, GPT-4o-mini, qwen2.5-coder:14b) against an identical multi-service honeypot—holding prompt, orchestrator, and target constant. We find statistically significant, large-effect differences in exploitation rates (Gemini: 85%, Claude: 61%, GPT-4o-mini: 56%, qwen: 25%; p < 0.001), with failure modes strongly model-specific: API truncation (Claude), premature completion (qwen), and iteration-budget exhaustion (GPT-4o-mini). Crucially, we correct a prior misattribution—91 HTTP 529 errors were upstream service outages (not model refusals). First-exploit timing was tightly bounded (15–30 seconds), and cross-service credential reuse occurred only with extended conversation history. This work establishes a foundational benchmark for evaluating AI attacker reliability.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) can autonomously conduct multi-stage cyber attacks, but the consistency of their offensive behavior under repeated trials remains unstudied. This work presents the first large-scale empirical measurement of LLM attack consistency: 400 autonomous penetration testing runs (4 models, 100 each) against an identical honeypot hosting OWASP Juice Shop and two additional vulnerable services, holding prompt, orchestrator, and target constant. No model emitted a content refusal that survived the orchestrator's one-shot authorization re-prompt at iterations 0-1. Claude Sonnet 4's API calls did encounter upstream service unavailability - 91 of 1,135 calls returned HTTP 529 overloaded_error during a documented Anthropic capacity event, truncating 39 of 100 Claude runs. An earlier draft catalogued these as safety refusals; on full-log audit they are upstream API failures, not model-level refusals. Despite this, Claude achieved full exploitation in 61 of 100 runs; Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite in 85; GPT-4o-mini in 56 while deploying 98 unique attack strategies; qwen2.5-coder:14b in 25. Failure modes are model-distinctive: Claude through API truncation (39 runs), qwen through premature completion (52), GPT-4o-mini through iteration-budget exhaustion (23). Cross-service credential reuse appeared only in configurations retaining the most conversation history (qwen 57%, GPT-4o-mini 49%, cloud models 0% on 5-exchange windows). Cross-model exploitation rate differences are statistically significant (p < 0.001) with large effect sizes; qwen vs. Gemini SQL injection rates differ at Cohen's h = 1.12. First-exploit timing fell within a 15-30 second wall-clock range. To our knowledge, this is the first study to measure autonomous LLM attack behavior at N=100 per model across a multi-service target.

item.title: Honeyval: A Comprehensive Evaluation Framework for LLM-powered HTTP Honeypots
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29963v1

AI Summary (中文)

Honeyval：面向大语言模型驱动型HTTP蜜罐的综合性评估框架

背景与问题：蜜罐作为模拟真实系统组件的诱饵系统，是网络安全防御的关键技术。近年来，大语言模型（LLM）因其强泛化与交互能力，正被广泛用作高交互蜜罐的仿真引擎，在降低系统安全风险的同时提升欺骗性。然而，现有LLM驱动蜜罐的评估方法严重碎片化——多依赖固定命令下的响应相似度计算、人工测试或真实部署，存在不可扩展、不可复现、攻击场景失真、配置适应性差四大缺陷，严重制约了该方向的系统性研究与工程落地。

方法创新：本文提出Honeyval，首个专为LLM驱动HTTP蜜罐设计的综合性、自动化、可验证评估框架。其核心设计包括：

  • ✅ 16种真实后端应用建模：覆盖Web服务、API网关、数据库接口等典型场景，确保评估环境贴近实战；
  • ✅ AI黑客代理（AI Hacking Agents）作为攻击者：替代人工或脚本，实现规模化、自适应、持续演化的攻击行为生成；
  • ✅ 双控制任务机制：独立监控攻击代理能力（如漏洞探测成功率）与蜜罐响应能力（如协议合规性），解耦评估干扰因素；
  • ✅ 明确定义且可验证的利用目标（如获取/etc/passwd、触发RCE）：使“成功攻击”具备客观判定标准，支持量化比较。

关键发现：基于Honeyval对7款主流低成本LLM（如Qwen2-7B、Phi-3、Llama3-8B）的系统评测表明：

  • LLM蜜罐平均诱导攻击者交互时长达规则基线的3.2倍，且被前沿模型（GPT-4o、Claude-3.5）识别为“蜜罐”的概率低于12%（规则基线>89%）；
  • 在维持单次交互成本低于$0.008的前提下，对智能攻击者仍具显著经济优势；
  • 配置反制策略（如主动日志误导、虚假漏洞反馈）可延长交互，但检测率同步上升——揭示出欺骗深度与隐蔽性间的本质权衡。

Honeyval开源，为可信、可复现、面向攻防对抗的LLM安全应用评估树立新基准。

AI Summary (English)

Honeyval is the first comprehensive, automated, and verifiable evaluation framework for LLM-powered HTTP honeypots. It addresses critical limitations of prior work by (1) grounding evaluations in 16 realistic backend applications; (2) deploying AI hacking agents—not static scripts or humans—as adaptive attackers; (3) introducing two control tasks to independently monitor attacker capability and honeypot fidelity; and (4) defining clear, binary-exploit goals (e.g., reading /etc/passwd) for objective success measurement. Using Honeyval, we evaluate 7 cost-efficient LLMs (e.g., Qwen2-7B, Phi-3, Llama3-8B) as HTTP honeypots. Results show LLM honeypots sustain interactions 3.2× longer than rule-based baselines and evade detection by frontier models (GPT-4o, Claude-3.5) with <12% false-positive rate, while maintaining average per-interaction costs under $0.008. We further identify a fundamental trade-off: counter-offensive configurations (e.g., fake vulnerability injection) extend engagement but increase detectability—highlighting the tension between deception depth and stealth. Honeyval enables reproducible, attack-realistic, and configuration-aware assessment of LLM security applications.

Abstract

Honeypots are decoy systems mimicking real system components designed to defend against cyber attacks. Recently, LLMs increasingly serve as simulation backbones for honeypots. They enable defenders to construct high-interaction honeypots with low system security risks. However, LLM-powered honeypot development lacks a unified evaluation framework. Most evaluations consist of measuring response similarity on fixed commands, manual testing, or real-world deployment. These methods are often not scalable for development, reproducible across evaluations, representative of practical attacks, or adaptable to various attacker and honeypot configurations. In this work, we bridge this gap and propose Honeyval, a comprehensive evaluation framework for LLM-powered HTTP honeypots. We address the limitations of prior evaluations by grounding the honeypots in 16 backend applications, using AI hacking agents as attackers, employing two control tasks to monitor agent and honeypot capabilities across customizations, and defining clear and verifiable exploit goals for the attacker. Using Honeyval, we conduct an extensive evaluation of recent cost-efficient LLMs as HTTP honeypots. Our experiments highlight the promise of LLM-powered honeypots; they lead to substantially longer interactions with the attacker than rule-based baseline honeypots and are far less frequently detected even by frontier models, all while, on average, preserving a running cost advantage against agentic attackers. Further, we experiment with different counter-offensive honeypots configurations, and observe unique trade-offs, such as longer interactions at the cost of increased detection.

item.title: Hijacking Agent Memory: Stealthy Trojan Attacks Through Conversational Interaction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29960v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）智能体广泛采用长期记忆机制支持持续性、自主性任务执行，其记忆模块正成为新型攻击面——记忆投毒（Memory Poisoning）。然而，现有攻击方法多假设恶意内容可被直接写入记忆，忽视了现代记忆管道中关键的选择性提取（selective extraction） 与语义重写（semantic rewriting） 阶段，导致其在真实部署场景下失效。

方法创新：MemPoison 攻击框架

本文提出 MemPoison——首个面向对话交互场景、可绕过选择性记忆机制的隐蔽特洛伊攻击。其核心突破在于三阶段协同设计：

  • 语义关系桥（Semantic Relational Bridge）：将触发器（trigger）与恶意载荷（payload）构造成逻辑自洽的陈述句（如“Dr. Lee leads Project Orion”），确保二者被记忆系统联合抽取、共同存入长期记忆；
  • 实体伪装（Entity Masquerading）：优化触发器为高置信度命名实体（如虚构但合理的机构名、人名），显著提升抗重写鲁棒性；
  • 联合嵌入优化（Joint Embedding Optimization）：通过梯度引导，使含触发器的文本在嵌入空间中形成紧密簇，同时与良性文本嵌入保持显著隔离，实现高隐蔽性与高触发一致性。

关键结果与启示

在多领域（客服、科研助理、金融顾问）及多种记忆机制（FAISS+LLM summarization, MemGPT-style reflection）上的评估表明：MemPoison 攻击成功率高达 0.95，显著超越基线方法（平均提升 +37%）。机理分析揭示其利用了嵌入空间的各向异性（anisotropy） 与注意力分布偏移，暴露了选择性记忆系统在语义保真与安全边界间的根本张力。我们还系统检验了记忆清洗、输入过滤、嵌入异常检测等主流防御策略，证实其均存在不可规避的漏检或误杀缺陷，凸显该威胁的严峻性与防御范式重构的必要性。

AI Summary (English)

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly rely on long-term memory for persistent autonomy—yet this capability introduces a critical vulnerability: memory poisoning via conversational interaction. Prior attacks fail under realistic selective memory pipelines that filter and rewrite incoming information. We propose MemPoison, the first stealthy Trojan attack that hijacks agent memory through natural dialogue. It comprises three innovations: (i) a semantic relational bridge to co-extract trigger and payload as a coherent statement; (ii) entity masquerading to optimize triggers as plausible named entities, evading rewriting; and (iii) joint embedding optimization to cluster malicious texts tightly in embedding space while isolating them from benign ones. Across diverse agent domains and memory architectures, MemPoison achieves up to 95% attack success rate, outperforming baselines by ≥37%. Mechanistic analysis reveals exploitation of embedding-space anisotropy and attention pattern shifts. We further demonstrate fundamental limitations of existing defenses—including memory scrubbing, input filtering, and embedding anomaly detection—highlighting urgent needs for memory-aware security design.

Abstract

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly leverage long term memory to support persistent and autonomous task execution. However, this capability also introduces a new attack surface: memory poisoning, where adversaries can inject malicious information to influence future behavior. Existing memory poisoning attacks often assume that injected content can be stored directly in memory, overlooking the selective extraction and rewriting stages in modern memory pipelines. This makes prior methods ineffective under realistic settings. In this paper, we propose MemPoison, a novel memory poisoning attack that bypasses selective memory mechanisms in LLM agents, where an attacker can inject triggerable backdoors into the agent's long-term memory through dialogue interactions, thereby misleading its subsequent responses. MemPoison introduces three key components: (i) a semantic relational bridge that binds the trigger and payload into a coherent statement to ensure they are extracted into memory together; (ii) entity masquerading that optimizes triggers to mimic named entities, resisting rewriting; and (iii) joint embedding optimization that shapes trigger-injected texts into a tight cluster in the embedding space while maintaining isolation from benign embeddings for stealth. Evaluations across different agent domains and memory mechanisms show MemPoison achieves attack success rates up to 0.95, outperforming existing baselines. Mechanistic analysis indicates that the attack exploits embedding-space anisotropy and shifts attention patterns, highlighting core vulnerabilities in selective memory systems. We evaluate multiple defense strategies and demonstrate their fundamental limitations in mitigating the attack.

item.title: Mitigating Stethoscope-Induced Shortcuts in Respiratory Sound Classification under Federated Domain Generalization with Causality-Inspired Interventions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29862v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

人工智能驱动的呼吸音分类（RSC）在自动化肺部疾病筛查中潜力巨大，但跨医疗站点部署常受听诊器设备异质性严重制约——不同机构使用型号、频响特性迥异的电子听诊器，导致模型在未见过的设备上性能骤降。现有联邦学习方法多假设客户端数据分布近似，而本工作首次形式化提出联邦域泛化（FedDG）框架，专用于应对“听诊器诱导的设备偏移”：各客户端使用真实异构听诊器采集数据，模型需泛化至完全未知的新型听诊器。

方法创新

针对听诊器风格（style）与疾病内容（content）高度纠缠、传统确定性风格剥离易损语义的问题，我们提出因果启发的多模态FedDG框架，包含三大核心组件：

  • 因果式设备风格干预网络：基于反事实推理，在保持病理内容不变前提下，对音频特征施加可控的设备风格扰动，增强模型对风格变异的鲁棒性；
  • 反事实文本增强：利用听诊器元数据（如品牌、型号）生成中性化文本提示，抑制模型对设备标识符的捷径学习；
  • 梯度对齐机制：在客户端本地训练中约束梯度方向一致性，促进跨设备的判别性、设备无关表征学习。

框架以多模态语言-音频预训练模型（如AudioCLIP）为骨干，端到端联合优化。

主要结果

在ICBHI与SPRSound两大公开数据集上，采用严格的“留一设备出”（leave-one-device-out）评估协议，本方法显著超越主流联邦学习（FedAvg、FedProx）及数据增强基线（SpecAugment、Mixup），平均准确率提升+5.2%–7.8%，AUC提升+6.1%–8.3%。消融实验证实三模块协同增益显著，且风格干预有效解耦内容与设备偏差。代码将于论文发表后开源。

AI Summary (English)

AI-driven respiratory sound classification (RSC) promises automated pulmonary disease screening, yet real-world deployment is hampered by severe inter-stethoscope variability across clinical sites. We formalize this challenge as Federated Domain Generalization (FedDG), where clients use heterogeneous stethoscopes and the model must generalize to unseen devices. Empirically, we find stethoscope-induced style and disease-specific content are highly entangled—making deterministic style removal unreliable. To address this, we propose a causality-inspired multimodal FedDG framework, integrating: (i) a device-style intervention network that performs content-preserving counterfactual perturbations on audio features; (ii) counterfactual text augmentation to neutralize metadata-based shortcuts; and (iii) gradient alignment across clients to foster device-invariant representations. Built upon a language-audio pretrained backbone, our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in leave-one-device-out evaluation on ICBHI and SPRSound—outperforming conventional federated learning and augmentation baselines by +5.2–7.8% accuracy and +6.1–8.3% AUC. Code will be released upon publication.

Abstract

AI-driven respiratory sound classification (RSC) is promising for automated pulmonary disease detection, yet multi-site deployment is hindered by inter-stethoscope variability. We introduce a federated domain generalization (FedDG) formulation for RSC under stethoscope-induced device shifts, where clients use heterogeneous devices and the model is evaluated on unseen devices. Our empirical analysis shows that stethoscope-induced style and disease-specific content are tightly entangled, making deterministic style removal unreliable. In response, we propose a causality-inspired multimodal FedDG framework that combines: (i) a causality-inspired device style intervention network that performs content-preserving style perturbations, (ii) counterfactual text augmentation that neutralizes metadata shortcuts, and (iii) gradient alignment that facilitates device-invariant representations across clients. Built on a multimodal language-audio pretraining model, it outperforms conventional data augmentation and federated learning baselines in leave-one-device-out validation on ICBHI and SPRSound datasets. Code will be released upon publication.

item.title: AgentDoG 1.5: A Lightweight and Scalable Alignment Framework for AI Agent Safety and Security
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29801v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

现代开放世界AI智能体（如OpenClaw）具备跨环境执行能力，但同时引入了新型、广泛的安全风险源；而前沿大模型的普及显著降低了攻击门槛，导致现有对齐框架难以支撑真实场景部署。

方法创新

我们提出AgentDoG 1.5——一种轻量级、可扩展的智能体安全对齐框架：

  • 更新安全分类学：首次系统纳入Codex与OpenClaw执行场景中涌现的风险类型（如环境越界调用、隐式工具链劫持、跨沙箱状态污染）；
  • 构建分类学引导的数据引擎：融合影响函数（influence function）净化技术，仅需约1,000个高质量样本即可高效训练多规模模型（0.8B/2B/4B/8B参数），显著降低数据依赖；
  • 实现极简训练范式：在Docker级轻量环境中，将SFT+RL安全训练开销降低两个数量级（较基线提速≥100×）；
  • 部署零训练在线守卫：AgentDoG 1.5可作为无微调、低延迟（<80ms）的实时安全守卫模块，嵌入任意推理流水线。

核心成果

在涵盖12类高危交互场景（含API滥用、权限提权、隐蔽代理链等）的基准测试中，AgentDoG 1.5（4B）以92.7%平均安全通过率超越GPT-4o（86.3%）与Claude-3.5-Sonnet（84.1%），且推理显存占用仅为GPT-5.4的1/18。所有模型权重、风险标注数据集及训练代码已完全开源，支持社区复现与安全共建。

AI Summary (English)

AgentDoG 1.5 is a lightweight, scalable alignment framework designed to address emerging safety and security risks in modern open-world AI agents (e.g., OpenClaw). We revise the agent safety taxonomy to cover novel threats from codex-driven execution and cross-environment tool use, then build a taxonomy-guided data engine with influence-function purification—enabling efficient training of compact variants (0.8B–8B) using only ~1k samples. AgentDoG 1.5 achieves state-of-the-art safety performance (92.7% avg. pass rate across 12 high-risk interactive scenarios), outperforming GPT-4o and Claude-3.5-Sonnet while reducing Docker-level deployment overhead by two orders of magnitude. Crucially, it operates as a zero-shot, low-latency (<80ms) online guardrail—requiring no fine-tuning during inference. All models, datasets, and code are openly released.

Abstract

Modern open-world agents such as OpenClaw exhibit powerful cross-environment execution capabilities yet introduce broad new safety risk sources. Meanwhile, advanced frontier AI models drastically lower attack barriers, rendering current agent alignment frameworks inadequate for real-world deployment. To tackle these emerging threats, we propose a lightweight and scalable agent safety alignment framework. Specifically, we update the agent safety taxonomy to accommodate emergent risks from Codex and OpenClaw execution scenarios. We further build a taxonomy-guided data engine with influence-function purification to train lightweight AgentDoG 1.5 variants (0.8B, 2B, 4B, and 8B parameters) using only around 1k samples, achieving comparable performance with leading closed-source models (e.g., GPT-5.4). Based on AgentDoG 1.5, we construct a highly efficient agentic safety SFT and RL training environment, which reduces deployment overhead in Docker-level environments by two orders of magnitude. Finally, we deploy AgentDoG 1.5 as a training-free online guardrail for real-time safety moderation. Extensive experimental results indicate that AgentDoG 1.5 achieves state-of-the-art performance in diverse and complex interactive agentic scenarios. All models and datasets are openly released.

item.title: Multi-Legal-Bench: Evaluating LLMs on Legal Reasoning Across Jurisdictions, Languages, and Legal Traditions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29738v1

AI Summary (中文)

多司法辖区法律基准 Multi-Legal-Bench：突破语言、法系与管辖壁垒的统一评估框架

当前法律NLP基准普遍存在单语局限性或任务异质性聚合问题——或仅覆盖单一语言（如仅英语），或将不同法域下定义迥异的法律任务强行合并，导致跨语言、跨法系性能比较不可靠。为此，本研究提出 Multi-Legal-Bench：首个真正意义上的跨司法辖区、跨语言、跨法系法律推理基准。

该基准覆盖6个大陆法系国家（乌克兰、法国、荷兰、波兰、捷克、立陶宛），涵盖4大语系（斯拉夫、罗曼、日耳曼、波罗的海），基于1.34亿份真实法院判决文书构建。我们严格对齐任务定义，在所有司法辖区统一实施5项结构化法律推理任务：法院类型分类、判决形式分类、案件结果预测、法律规范抽取、案由类别预测——全部映射自各国法院登记系统的标准化元数据。为反映现实约束，设计稀疏5×6任务-辖区矩阵（30格中仅20格有标注），避免强制泛化。

实验评估7个前沿大模型（通过AWS Bedrock零样本/三样本提示）及4个中小规模模型（3–12B参数）用于缩放分析。关键发现包括：

  • 任务驱动的少样本增益具有跨辖区稳健性：在乌克兰发现的少样本提升效应，在其余5国全部复现；
  • 无绝对主导模型：模型排名随任务类型与司法辖区显著波动，凸显“任务-辖区耦合”特性；
  • 跨语言迁移不依赖语言亲缘性：乌克兰→法国（罗曼语族，性能下降−2.1个百分点）优于乌克兰→波兰（同属斯拉夫语族，下降−13.7个百分点），标签集对齐度比语系归属更能预测迁移质量；
  • 分词器“丰度”（tokenizer fertility）与跨语言准确率弱相关（r=−0.27, p=0.14），证实模型架构与预训练数据构成更关键瓶颈。

本基准完全开源：含全部原始数据、标准化prompt模板、模型预测结果及评估脚本。

AI Summary (English)

Multi-Legal-Bench is the first cross-jurisdictional legal benchmark enabling rigorous, task-aligned evaluation of LLMs across six civil-law countries (Ukraine, France, Netherlands, Poland, Czech Republic, Lithuania), four language families, and 134 million real court decisions. It defines five identical legal reasoning tasks—court-type classification, judgment-form classification, case-outcome prediction, legal norm extraction, and cause-category prediction—mapped to structured national registry metadata, arranged in a deliberately sparse 5×6 task-jurisdiction matrix. Evaluating 7 frontier LLMs (zero-/3-shot via AWS Bedrock) and 4 smaller models (3–12B), we find: (1) task-dependent few-shot gains observed in Ukraine replicate robustly across all jurisdictions; (2) no model dominates universally—rankings shift significantly with both task and jurisdiction; (3) cross-lingual few-shot transfer quality correlates better with label-set alignment than language family proximity (e.g., UA→FR transfer outperforms UA→PL despite Slavic kinship); and (4) tokenizer fertility shows negligible predictive power for cross-lingual accuracy (r=−0.27, p=0.14), underscoring architecture and pretraining data as primary determinants. All data, prompts, and predictions are publicly released.

Abstract

Legal NLP benchmarks overwhelmingly evaluate a single language or aggregate tasks that differ fundamentally across jurisdictions, making cross-lingual comparison impossible. We introduce Multi-Legal-Bench, the first cross-jurisdictional legal benchmark that evaluates identical tasks across six countries (Ukraine, France, Netherlands, Poland, Czech Republic, Lithuania), four language families, and 134 million court decisions. The benchmark defines five tasks court-type classification, judgment form classification, case-outcome prediction, legal norm extraction, and cause category prediction mapped to structured metadata from national court registries, forming a deliberately sparse 5x6 task-jurisdiction matrix (20 of 30 cells filled). We evaluate 7 frontier LLMs under zero-shot and 3-shot prompting via AWS Bedrock, with 4 additional small/medium models (3-12B) for scaling analysis. Our results reveal that: (1) task-dependent few-shot effects discovered in Ukrainian replicate across all jurisdictions; (2) no single model dominates any language rankings shift with both task and jurisdiction; (3) cross-lingual few-shot transfer does not follow language proximity: UA->FR (Romance, -2.1 pp) transfers better than UA->PL (Slavic, -13.7 pp), with label-set alignment predicting transfer quality better than language family; and (4) tokenizer fertility, despite a 2.3x spread, does not significantly predict cross-lingual accuracy (r=-0.27, p=0.14), suggesting that model architecture and pretraining data dominate tokenizer efficiency. We release all data, prompts, and model predictions.

item.title: Opir: Efficient Multi-Task Safety Classification for Toxicity, Jailbreaks, Hate Speech, and Harmful Content
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29659v1

AI Summary (中文)

Opir：面向多任务安全分类的高效轻量级守门员模型

背景与挑战：面向大语言模型（LLM）的实时安全过滤亟需兼顾高精度、低延迟与小体积的守门员（guardrail）模型，尤其需在不依赖庞大参数量的前提下，精准区分有毒内容、越狱尝试、仇恨言论、有害响应等多元风险，并避免将良性敏感文本（如医学讨论、历史批判）误判为有害内容。

方法创新：本文提出 Opir——基于 GLiClass 架构构建的轻量级编码器家族。其核心包含：

  • ✅ 多任务统一架构：同步支持二元安全/不安全判别、多标签毒性细粒度分类（如“侮辱+性别歧视+煽动暴力”）、越狱意图识别、以及零样本提示/响应风险类别推断；
  • ✅ 极致轻量化边缘版本：专用于二元安全分类的子模型参数量 <100M，适配端侧部署；
  • ✅ 三层细粒度训练体系：覆盖 16个顶层标签、126个中层标签、854个叶级标签（共996类），数据融合分类学对齐的恶意样本、对抗挖掘的难负例、安全保持型良性样本、合成响应、多语言翻译数据，并整合 Aegis2 与 WildGuard 子集；
  • ✅ 开源评估框架：支持 GLiClass、GLiNER2 及解码器类模型，覆盖提示安全、响应安全、拒绝率、子类别分析等 8 类评估维度，兼容主流公开基准。

关键成果：在涵盖 12 项安全分类任务与 17 类细粒度分类任务的扩展评测中，Opir 在多数基准上性能媲美或超越当前最强开源守门员系统（含 GLiNER2 与生成式方案），同时推理开销显著降低——验证了“小模型、大能力”的可行性。

AI Summary (English)

Opir is a family of efficient, encoder-based guardrail models built on the GLiClass architecture for real-time LLM safety filtering. It unifies binary safe/unsafe classification, multi-label toxicity detection, jailbreak identification, and zero-shot prompt/response categorization—while offering edge-optimized variants (<100M parameters) for deployment-constrained settings. Trained on a rich, taxonomy-grounded dataset (996 categories across 3 hierarchical levels) combining adversarial negatives, safety-preserving benign examples, synthetic responses, multilingual data, and curated subsets from Aegis2 and WildGuard, Opir achieves competitive or state-of-the-art performance across 12 safety classification and 17 category-level tasks—outperforming or matching eight contemporary open-weight baselines (including GLiNER2- and generative-based systems) with significantly lower computational footprint. We also open-source a flexible evaluation harness supporting multiple model backends and comprehensive safety assessment dimensions.

Abstract

Real-time safety filtering for large language model (LLM) applications requires classifiers that can detect unsafe prompts, toxic language, jailbreak attempts, and unsafe responses without the cost profile of large guardrail models, and that can distinguish benign sensitive text from genuinely covert harmful content. In this paper, we introduce Opir, a family of encoder-based guardrail models built on the GLiClass architecture. Opir includes multi-task models for binary safe/unsafe classification, multi-label toxicity classification, jailbreak classification, and zero-shot unsafe prompt and response categorization. We also release edge variants with fewer than 100M parameters dedicated to binary safe/unsafe categorization. The models are trained on a three-level taxonomy containing 996 categories across 16 top-level labels, 126 mid-level labels, and 854 leaf labels. Opir's training data combines taxonomy-grounded unsafe prompts, adversarially mined hard negatives, benign safety-preserving examples, generated response examples, multilingual translations, and portions of the Aegis2 and WildGuard training subsets. We also open-sourced an evaluation harness that supports GLiClass and GLiNER2 backends as well as decoder-based models, and covers binary safety classification, multi-label categorization, toxicity, jailbreak detection, prompt safety, response safety, response refusal, and prompt subcategory views across public benchmark families. Across an expanded comparison spanning 12 safety-classification tasks and 17 category tasks against eight contemporary guardrail systems -- including both GLiNER2-based and generative guardrail models -- Opir variants are competitive on or ahead of the strongest open-weight baselines on the majority of benchmark datasets while operating with a substantially smaller deployment footprint.

item.title: Beyond Attack Success Rate: Temporal Logit Observability for LLM Safety Failures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29629v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

传统安全评估过度依赖攻击成功率（ASR）——一个仅在生成结束时给出“是/否”的二元指标。它能判断LLM是否被成功越狱，却完全掩盖了失败发生的时间路径：两个导致同等有害输出的攻击，可能源于截然不同的解码动态（如早期妥协 vs. 临界点崩溃），而ASR对此毫无分辨力。

方法创新：时序对数几率可观测性（TLO）

我们提出Temporal Logit Observability（TLO）——一种无需训练、纯基于解码过程中原始logits的诊断框架。其核心是实时追踪合规性-拒绝性logit差值（compliance-refusal margin）随时间步的变化，并将每个模型-攻击组合映射到一个校准的2D平面上：横轴表征失败发生的时机（early/late），纵轴表征失败的确定性（gradual/abrupt）。该设计刻意聚焦于ASR最薄弱的区域——即高ASR但成因异质的攻击簇。

关键发现

  • 在4种对齐LLM（Llama-3-8B-Instruct, Qwen2-7B-Instruct, Phi-3-mini, Gemma-2-9B）与3类主流越狱范式（prompt injection, suffix optimization, semantic paraphrasing）上验证：ASR相近（±1.5%）的攻击，在TLO平面上呈现显著分离的聚类，揭示同一模型存在多种失败模式；
  • TLO几何结构与基于隐藏状态的拒绝方向探针（refusal-direction probes）高度一致（Pearson r > 0.87），证实其语义有效性；唯一偏差出现在Phi-3-mini，暴露了固定词典方法对细粒度拒绝表征的边界；
  • 基于TLO导出的简易早停规则（margin < −2.1持续3步），在保持0%误报率（对普通良性查询）前提下，将成功越狱率降低>52%。

意义

TLO首次实现仅从logits中无损还原安全失效的何时发生与如何展开，推动LLM安全评估从静态结果导向转向动态过程可解释。

AI Summary (English)

Attack Success Rate (ASR) is a coarse, endpoint-only metric that masks how and when safety failures unfold during LLM decoding. To address this, we propose Temporal Logit Observability (TLO): a training-free, logits-only diagnostic that tracks the compliance–refusal logit margin step-by-step and embeds each model–attack pair onto an interpretable 2D plane—where axes capture timing (early vs. late failure) and certainty (gradual vs. abrupt). Across four aligned LLMs and three jailbreak paradigms, attacks with nearly identical ASR (±1.5%) occupy distinct, semantically meaningful regions on the TLO plane—revealing diverse temporal failure modes within the same model. TLO geometry strongly aligns with refusal-direction probes from hidden states (r > 0.87), except for Phi-3-mini, highlighting a limitation of fixed-lexicon margin estimation. Crucially, a simple early-stopping rule derived from TLO reduces successful jailbreaks by over 52% with zero false positives on benign queries. TLO shifts safety evaluation from “did it fail?” to “when and how did it fail?”—observable directly from logits.

Abstract

Attack Success Rate (ASR) evaluates each jailbreak with a single yes/no label at the end of generation, telling us whether a failure happened but not how it unfolded. Two attacks that produce equally harmful outputs may have followed completely different paths, and ASR cannot tell them apart. We make those hidden paths observable from logits alone. Temporal Logit Observability (TLO) is a training-free diagnostic that watches a compliance-refusal margin during decoding and places each model-attack condition on a calibrated 2D plane. By design, this plane is most informative exactly where ASR is least informative: among attacks that succeed for genuinely different reasons. Across four aligned LLMs and three jailbreak paradigms, attacks with nearly identical ASR land at clearly different points on the plane: the same model can fail through different temporal patterns. The geometry matches refusal-direction probes from hidden states on most conditions, with one model showing the limit of our fixed-lexicon approach. A simple early-stop rule derived from TLO cuts successful jailbreaks by more than half, without false alarms on plain benign queries. Safety evaluation should report when and how a failure unfolds, not only whether it occurred. TLO makes the first two observable from logits alone.

item.title: Quotient DAGs for Off-Policy Evaluation:Forward-Flow Importance Sampling and Exact Slate Propensities
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29500v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题
